outbound_official.notes: The Visa Advice Unit within the Consular Services Office of Malta's Ministry for Foreign and European Affairs operates an interactive per-country lookup tool at consularplus.gov.mt/visa-advice for Maltese ordinary passport holders. The site is a fully JavaScript-rendered SPA; no static downloadable country list is published. Country-level data cannot be scraped from this endpoint without a JS-capable browser; lists above are left empty pending a browser-based extraction. The tool replaced the older foreignandeu.gov.mt/en/Pages/Visa-Advice-for-Maltese-nationals.aspx page (now redirects to foreign.gov.mt). Contact: consular.mfea@gov.mt, tel +356 2204 2313 / 2204 2331.

visa_free.nationality: EU/EEA Member State nationals and Swiss nationals
visa_free.notes: All citizens of EU Member States (including other Schengen members) and EEA/Swiss nationals benefit from EU freedom of movement and have no visa requirement. Malta is a full Schengen member. The 29 Schengen Member States are: Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Czech Republic, Croatia, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovenia, Slovakia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland.
Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports. 90 days within any 180-day period.
Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports. 90 days within any 180-day period.
Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports. 90 days within any 180-day period.
Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports issued in line with ICAO standards. 90 days within any 180-day period.

Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports issued by Kosovo in line with ICAO standards. Effective 1 January 2024 under Regulation (EU) 2023/850. Kosovo is not universally recognised as a state. 90 days within any 180-day period.
Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports issued in line with ICAO standards. 90 days within any 180-day period.
Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports. 90 days within any 180-day period.

Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports. From 13 October 2024, this includes holders of Serbian passports issued by the Serbian Coordination Directorate (Koordinaciona uprava) - the previous exclusion was removed by Regulation (EU) 2024/2495. 90 days within any 180-day period.
Visa exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports issued in line with ICAO standards. 90 days within any 180-day period.

UK nationals (British citizens who are nationals of the UK for purposes of Union law) are visa-exempt. Also includes: British nationals (Overseas), British overseas territories citizens (BOTC), British overseas citizens (BOC), British protected persons (BPP), British subjects (BS). 90 days within any 180-day period.
Special Administrative Region of China. Visa exemption applies only to holders of a 'Hong Kong Special Administrative Region' passport. 90 days within any 180-day period.
Special Administrative Region of China. Visa exemption applies only to holders of a 'Regiao Administrativa Especial de Macau' passport. 90 days within any 180-day period.

Territorial authority not recognised as a state by all EU member states. Visa exemption applies only to holders of passports issued by Taiwan which include an identity card number. 90 days within any 180-day period.

visa_policy.notes: Malta is a full Schengen Area member. Short-stay visa policy is governed by EU Regulation 2018/1806 (consolidated to 30 December 2025). All EU/EEA/Swiss nationals have unrestricted freedom of movement. Third-country nationals from Annex II countries are visa-exempt for up to 90 days in any 180-day period. All other third-country nationals require a Schengen visa (short stay, up to 90 days) or a National D-visa (long stay, 91-365 days). No e-visa or visa-on-arrival facility exists - visa applications must be submitted at a Visa Application Centre or Maltese diplomatic mission. ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) is expected to start operations in Q4 2026 (as confirmed by the European Council on 5 March 2025); as of June 2026 it is not yet accepting applications; when launched it will apply to all visa-exempt third-country nationals requiring pre-travel authorisation (€20 fee, valid 3 years). Key amendments since 2023: (1) Kosovo added to Annex II effective 1 January 2024 (Regulation (EU) 2023/850); (2) Serbia Coordination Directorate passport exclusion removed effective 13 October 2024 (Regulation (EU) 2024/2495); (3) Vanuatu removed from Annex II and added to Annex I (visa required) effective 3 February 2025 (Regulation (EU) 2025/11); (4) Regulation (EU) 2025/2441 (in force December 2025) strengthened visa suspension mechanism procedures but did not alter any country's Annex I/II classification. Georgia ordinary passport holders remain in Annex II; from 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027 the Commission suspended visa-free travel only for Georgian holders of diplomatic, service, and official passports under the revised suspension mechanism - this does not affect ordinary passport holders.

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen residence permit (Type C short-stay or Type D long-stay visa / national long-stay visa issued by another Schengen state)

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding a valid residence permit or long-stay visa (Type D national visa) issued by any Schengen Member State are authorised to enter Malta (and other Schengen states) for transit or short stays without an additional Schengen visa, provided the total stay does not exceed 90 days in any 180-day period and all other entry conditions of Article 6(1) of the Schengen Borders Code are met (valid travel document, sufficient means, no security threat). Entry is for transit to reach the issuing Schengen state or for short-stay visits. Other conditions of Art.6(1) of Regulation (EU) 2016/399 still apply.

Citizens of the People's Republic of China holding valid diplomatic or service passports (excluding Chinese 'public affairs' service passports per Article X of the agreement) may enter, exit or transit Malta visa-free for up to 30 days. Members of diplomatic/consular missions during their term of posting are also covered. Stays beyond 30 days require application through diplomatic channels. Officials at vice-ministerial level or above / major-general rank or above must notify Malta 5 working days before travel for official purposes. Agreement signed in Valletta on 18 December 2007; entry into force per notification procedure under Article X.

Qatari nationals holding valid diplomatic or special passports are exempt from visa requirements for Malta under the Agreement between the Government of Malta and the Government of the State of Qatar for the Exemption of Visa Requirements for Diplomatic and Special Passport Holders. Signed 14 April 2010; ratified 10 December 2011; in force 30 December 2011. Exact max stay days and further conditions stated in the treaty text (PDF).

Pakistani nationals holding valid diplomatic or official passports are exempt from visa requirements for Malta under the Agreement between Malta and Pakistan on Diplomatic Visa Waiver Agreements (Memorandum of Understanding). Signed 15 July 2016; ratified 1 October 2019; in force 19 December 2019. Full conditions in treaty text.

Algerian nationals holding valid diplomatic passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (information communicated by Member States to the Commission, 11 July 2023).

Bolivian nationals holding valid diplomatic or service/official passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Egyptian nationals holding valid diplomatic, service/official or special passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Indian nationals holding valid diplomatic passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023). Note: OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholders are NOT covered by this rule; no OCI-specific conditional access rule for Malta was found on official sources.

Indonesian nationals holding valid diplomatic or service/official passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Jordanian nationals holding valid diplomatic passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Kazakhstani nationals holding valid diplomatic passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Moroccan nationals holding valid diplomatic or service/official passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Omani nationals holding valid diplomatic, service/official or special passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Philippine nationals holding valid diplomatic or service/official passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Thai nationals holding valid diplomatic or service/official passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Tunisian nationals holding valid diplomatic or service/official passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

Turkish nationals holding valid diplomatic or service/official passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for Malta under a national derogation communicated to the European Commission. Listed in EU Official Journal notice on national derogations from the visa requirement (2023).

cbi.notes: REPEALED as of July 2025. The Granting of Citizenship by Naturalisation for Exceptional Services by Direct Investment (ESDI) programme, regulated under S.L. 188.06, was repealed in July 2025 following the Court of Justice of the EU Grand Chamber ruling (Case C-181/23, judgment of 29 April 2025) which held that Malta's investor citizenship scheme violates EU law (Article 20 TFEU and Article 4(3) TEU) as it amounts to the commercialisation of EU citizenship without genuine links to the Member State. Malta has replaced it with a discretionary 'citizenship by merit' pathway (S.L. 188.04, Article 10(9) of the Maltese Citizenship Act, Chapter 188), which grants citizenship to individuals making exceptional contributions to Malta or humanity in fields such as science, innovation, culture, philanthropy, and sports. No fixed minimum investment amount applies; no property purchase or government contribution is required. This is a fully discretionary grant, not a CBI programme in the traditional investment sense. Malta continues to permit dual citizenship (since 10 February 2000). Source: CJEU press release cp250052en.pdf; multiple official and legal sources confirm ESDI ended July 2025.

programs.name: Malta Permanent Residence Programme (MPRP)
programs.notes: Available to non-EU, non-EEA, non-Swiss nationals. Grants permanent residency (not citizenship). UPDATED via Legal Notice 146 of 2025 (published 22 July 2025, effective for applications from 1 January 2025). Key financial requirements: (1) Administration fee: €60,000 (€15,000 non-refundable upfront within 1 month of submission + €45,000 within 2 months after Letter of Approval in Principle). (2) Government contribution: €37,000 (unified, regardless of whether applicant purchases or leases property; replaces previous dual structure of €28,000 purchase / €58,000 rental). Spouse and minor children no longer attract a separate contribution fee. (3) Property: Purchase qualifying property at minimum €375,000, OR lease at minimum €14,000/year; property must be held/maintained for at least 5 years. (4) NGO donation: €2,000 to a local registered NGO approved by Residency Malta Agency. (5) Proof of capital: Option A - €500,000 total assets (of which minimum €150,000 must be financial assets such as stocks, bonds, bank deposits); Option B - €650,000 total assets (of which minimum €75,000 must be in financial assets). Assets monitored annually for first 5 years. (6) Adult dependant fee: €7,500 each (excluding spouse and minor children). Ineligible: Afghanistan, North Korea, Iran, DRC, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Yemen, Venezuela, Russia, Belarus. Processing: decisions within a 'reasonable timeframe'. Regulated by S.L. 217.26. Residence certificate does not expire as long as obligations are maintained.

programs.name: Malta Residence and Visa Programme (MRVP)
programs.notes: Separate from the MPRP; grants Schengen travel rights with Maltese residency. Full investment amounts not extractable from public-facing official pages - see S.L. 217.18 and MRVP FAQs (PDF). Eligible for four generations of family members. Investment must be held for minimum 5 years.

programs.name: Global Residence Programme (GRP)
programs.notes: Tax-residency programme for non-EU, non-EEA, non-Swiss nationals not already long-term residents. Provides flat 15% tax rate on foreign-sourced income remitted to Malta (35% on locally-sourced income). Administrative fee: €5,500 if qualifying property is in South Malta; €6,000 otherwise. Applicants must purchase or lease a qualifying property in Malta. Regulated by Malta Tax & Customs Administration (MTCA, formerly CFR).

programs.name: Nomad Residence Permit
programs.notes: Available to non-EU/EEA/Swiss third-country nationals who work remotely. Minimum gross annual income: €42,000 (for applications from 1 April 2024 onwards; previous threshold was €32,400). Applicants must work for a foreign-registered employer under contract, or be a partner/shareholder of a foreign-registered company, or provide freelance/consulting services to foreign-based clients. Initial permit: 1 year; renewable up to 3 times (maximum 4 years total). Requires valid travel document, health insurance covering EU risks, property rental/purchase agreement, police certificate.

programs.name: Key Employee Initiative (KEI)
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals with offer for a managerial or highly technical position, OR startup roles endorsed by Malta Enterprise. Minimum annual gross salary: €45,000. Must provide certified qualifications, warrants or proof of work experience. Employer must advertise the role for at least 2 weeks within 2 months prior to application.
programs.processing_time: 5 working days from complete application submission
programs.notes: Initial permit valid 1 year; renewable up to 3 years. Fee: €600 first application, €150/year renewal. Managed by Identità (formerly Identity Malta).

programs.name: Specialist Employee Initiative (SEI)
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals not meeting KEI criteria. Minimum annual gross salary: €30,000. Requires MQF Level 6+ qualification OR lower-level qualification plus 3+ years relevant experience (proven via contracts/references).
programs.processing_time: Not specified (standard Single Permit process applies)

programs.name: EU Blue Card
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals with advanced professional qualifications and high-salary job offers in EU countries including Malta.
programs.notes: Issued under EU Blue Card Directive. Managed by Identità.

programs.name: Malta Startup Residence Programme
programs.eligibility: Non-EU, non-EEA, non-Swiss startup founders, co-founders, and core employees. Startup must be registered no longer than 7 years globally. Must engage in eligible sectors (manufacturing, software, health/biotech, eco, other innovative activities). Minimum paid-up share capital of €25,000 (plus €10,000 per additional co-founder beyond 4, max 6 co-founders). Core employees must earn at least €30,000 gross annually. Business plan must be approved by Malta Enterprise.
programs.notes: Founders/co-founders receive a 3-year permit renewable for 5 further years. Core employees receive a 3-year permit renewable for 3 further years. Application fee: €750/adult applicant for 3-year permit + €300 residence card issuance fee + €690 Jobsplus employment licence. Ineligible: nationals/close ties to Afghanistan, North Korea, Iran, DRC, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Yemen, Venezuela. Jointly administered by Residency Malta Agency and Malta Enterprise.

validation_notes: ADDED Kosovo (XKX) to visa_free: Regulation (EU) 2023/850 transferred Kosovo from Annex I to Annex II effective 1 January 2024. The prior record noted this as a gap; it is now corrected and sourced to the official EU regulation.

REMOVED Vanuatu (VUT) from visa_free: Regulation (EU) 2025/11 (effective ~3 February 2025) moved Vanuatu from Annex II (visa-free) to Annex I (visa required) due to investor citizenship scheme security concerns. Vanuatu entry deleted.

CORRECTED Serbia (SRB) notes: Regulation (EU) 2024/2495 (effective 13 October 2024) removed the exclusion of Serbian Coordination Directorate (Koordinaciona uprava) passport holders. All holders of Serbian biometric passports are now visa-free. Source updated to official EUR-Lex regulation.

UPDATED MPRP capital requirement in rbi notes: Added Option B (€650,000 total assets with minimum €75,000 in financial assets) alongside the existing Option A (€500,000 with €150,000 financial), confirmed from residencymalta.gov.mt January 2025 brochure search results.
UPDATED visa_policy.notes: Added ETIAS expected start (late 2026), summarized three key post-2023 Annex II amendments (Kosovo, Serbia, Vanuatu) with regulation references.
UPDATED visa_policy.source_urls: Added consolidated Regulation 2018/1806 (20251230), Regulation 2023/850 (Kosovo), Regulation 2024/2495 (Serbia), Regulation 2025/11 (Vanuatu).
UPDATED data_quality.gaps: Resolved the Kosovo gap (now recorded); updated Vanuatu status.

[2026-06-27] CORRECTED cbi: ESDI programme repealed July 2025 following CJEU Case C-181/23 (judgment 29 April 2025). Set has_program=false, cleared options/amounts. Replaced with note describing new discretionary 'citizenship by merit' route (S.L. 188.04) which has no fixed investment thresholds and is not a CBI programme. Source: curia.europa.eu cp250052en.pdf and multiple confirming sources.

[2026-06-27] UPDATED visa_policy.notes: Refined ETIAS timeline to Q4 2026 (European Council 5 March 2025 announcement); confirmed not yet launched as of June 2026. Added note on Regulation (EU) 2025/2441 (December 2025) strengthening visa suspension mechanism. Added note on Georgia diplomatic/service/official passport suspension effective 6 March 2026 (ordinary passport holders remain visa-free, no change to visa_free list).

[2026-06-27] VERIFIED visa_free list: Re-checked against EU Regulation 2018/1806 consolidated text (20250203 and 20251230). No countries added to or removed from ordinary-passport Annex II in 2025-2026 beyond Vanuatu (already removed). Venezuela confirmed in Annex II as of December 2025. All other visa_free entries confirmed unchanged.

[2026-06-27] VERIFIED Nauru: Listed in Annex II with condition 'from the date of entry into force of an agreement on visa exemption to be concluded with the EU' - agreement not yet in force; Nauru correctly excluded from visa_free list.
[2026-06-27] VERIFIED Fiji: Confirmed in Annex I (visa required), not in Annex II; correctly excluded from visa_free list.

[2026-06-29] CORRECTED rbi.MPRP min_amount: Updated from €40,000 to €60,000 (new admin fee under Legal Notice 146 of 2025, effective for applications from 1 January 2025). Full fee restructure: admin fee €60,000 (€15K upfront + €45K post-approval), government contribution unified at €37,000 (regardless of purchase/rental route, replaces former €28,000 purchase / €58,000 rental split), property purchase threshold raised to €375,000 (from €300,000/€350,000), property rental threshold raised to €14,000/year (from €10,000/€12,000), spouse/minor children now exempt from additional contribution. Source: multiple secondary legal sources citing S.L. 217.26 LN146/2025; official S.L. 217.26 PDF at residencymalta.gov.mt confirmed updated version exists but binary PDF not directly parseable.

[2026-06-29] VERIFIED visa_free list: No changes to Schengen Annex II for ordinary passport holders since the last run (2026-06-27). Vanuatu remains in Annex I (visa required) per Regulation (EU) 2025/11. Georgia ordinary passport holders remain in Annex II (only diplomatic/service/official passport holders suspended). No new countries added to or removed from Annex II in 2025-2026 beyond Vanuatu (already accounted for). Kosovo, Serbia confirmed unchanged.

[2026-06-29] VERIFIED Nomad Residence Permit minimum income: Confirmed €42,000/year for applications from 1 April 2024 onwards, per nomad.residencymalta.gov.mt/nomad-eligibility/ (live official page confirmed).
[2026-06-29] VERIFIED ETIAS: Confirmed still Q4 2026 expected launch; not yet operational as of June 2026. No change to eta[] list (empty) or ETIAS notes.
gaps: Visa-on-arrival: Malta has no visa-on-arrival facility; none to record.
E-visa: Malta has no e-visa system; none to record.

ETA: Malta has no ETA system currently; ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) expected to launch Q4 2026 and will apply to visa-exempt ordinary passport holders - not yet operational as of June 2026.
Full investment amounts for the Malta Residence and Visa Programme (MRVP / S.L. 217.18) not extractable from public official pages; amounts omitted from min_amount field.

MPRP processing time not specified with a number of days/months on official pages - only described as 'reasonable timeframe'. MPRP LN146/2025 fee changes confirmed via secondary legal/advisory sources (ccmalta.com, andersen.com, malta-citizenship.info citing S.L. 217.26 LN146 of 2025); the official S.L. 217.26 PDF (residencymalta.gov.mt) was fetched as a binary PDF that could not be parsed directly — cross-confirmation from official PDF text pending.

Malta citizenship by merit (post-July 2025): komunita.gov.mt page returned blank on 2026-06-27 fetch; cannot confirm current published criteria from official page. Information on merit route sourced from secondary legal/advisory sources confirming S.L. 188.04; has_program set to false (no CBI) pending an official confirmation of the exact new rules. Official URL to re-check: https://komunita.gov.mt/services/acquisition-of-citizenship/

Conditional access - passport_type: The EU OJ 2023 notice (CELEX:52023XC0927(01)) is the most recent official list of Malta national derogations checked; the 2022 notice additionally listed Angola, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cambodia, Iran, Mongolia, and Qatar diplomatic/service passports - these were not present in 2023 notice, suggesting removal or non-confirmation; not recorded here due to uncertainty about current status. Ghana bilateral visa waiver agreement referenced in press sources but no confirmed treaty page on foreign.gov.mt was accessible; not recorded.

Conditional access - Georgia diplomatic suspension (March 2026): The Commission suspended visa-free travel for Georgian holders of diplomatic, service, and official passports effective 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027. Georgia ordinary passport holders remain in Annex II visa_free. The suspension should be added to conditional_access as a temporary removal of Georgia diplomatic/service/official passport exemption - not yet recorded in conditional_access due to absence of a prior Georgia diplomatic entry to update.

Conditional access - credential: No official Malta or EU source was found granting visa-free or reduced-requirement entry to Malta based solely on holding a valid US, UK, Australian, Canadian, or other non-Schengen visa or residence permit. No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder-specific rule for entry to Malta found on any official source.
Conditional access - special_status: No Malta-specific OCI or other diaspora/heritage special status entry rules were found on official sources.
Max stay days for Qatar (QAT) and Pakistan (PAK) diplomatic passport treaty entries: full treaty PDF texts not successfully extracted; max_stay_days set to null pending PDF access.

A more recent version of the Identità visa-free country list PDF may have been issued after October 2023; the official PDF URL used as primary source has not changed but may not reflect post-2023 amendments (Kosovo, Serbia Coord. Dir., Vanuatu) - those are sourced directly from EU regulations.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C)
visa_types.purpose: Authorises third-country nationals to visit Malta (and other Schengen states) for tourism, leisure or family visits. Does not permit employment or academic study.
visa_types.notes: Valid for up to 90 days within any 180-day period. Covers travel throughout the entire Schengen Zone. Standard Schengen visa fee is €80 (approx. USD 90 at time of research); reduced or waived for certain categories (children 6-12: €40; children under 6: free; students and researchers: €40 in some cases). Single-entry or multiple-entry issued based on applicant's travel history and need. Applications submitted through External Service Provider (VFS) or at a Maltese diplomatic mission. Fee Ordinance L.N.287 of 2020 applies. Malta is a full Schengen member state.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C) - Business Purpose
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to enter Malta for business meetings, conferences, negotiations or commercial activities for up to 90 days. Does not permit taking up employment.
visa_types.notes: Same Schengen Type C visa as the tourist visa; purpose declared at application as 'business'. Standard fee €80 (~USD 90). Applications via VFS or Maltese diplomatic mission. Must demonstrate ties to home country and intention to leave within 90 days.

visa_types.name: National Long-Stay Visa (Type D) - Student
visa_types.purpose: Issued to third-country nationals (including visa-exempt nationals) wishing to enrol in courses in Malta exceeding 90 calendar days.
visa_types.notes: A National D-Visa (long-stay) valid for up to 365 days. Required for any course exceeding 90 calendar days even for visa-exempt nationals. Educational institutions must maintain attendance records and report non-arrivals to Identità. Applications submitted through External Service Provider (VFS). Fee Ordinance L.N.287 of 2020 applies; specific fees not published on official page - contact Central Visa Unit at visa.identita@gov.mt or +356 2590 4550.

visa_types.name: National Long-Stay Visa (Type D) - Employment
visa_types.purpose: Issued to third-country nationals who have obtained an employment permit to work in Malta for a period exceeding 90 days.
visa_types.notes: National D-Visa (long-stay) valid up to 365 days, issued alongside or after obtaining a single permit or employment licence from Identità's Expatriates Unit. Typically issued for the period of the employment permit. Fee Ordinance L.N.287 of 2020 applies. Applications submitted through Central Visa Unit or Maltese diplomatic mission. Separate from the fast-track Key Employee Initiative (KEI) or Specialist Employee Initiative (SEI) single permit processes.

visa_types.name: Nomad Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-EU/EEA/Swiss nationals who work remotely using telecommunications technologies - for a foreign employer or as a freelancer/consultant with foreign clients - to reside in Malta for up to one year, renewable.
visa_types.notes: Initial permit: 1 year, renewable up to 3 times (maximum 4 years total in Malta). Minimum gross annual income: €42,000 (threshold effective 1 April 2024). Applicant must work for a foreign-registered employer under contract, or be a partner/shareholder of a foreign-registered company, or provide freelance/consulting services to foreign-based clients. Requires: valid travel document, health insurance covering EU risks, property rental/purchase agreement in Malta, police clearance certificate. Applications submitted online at portal.residencymalta.gov.mt. Administered by Residency Malta Agency. Not available to EU/EEA/Swiss nationals who have right of free movement.

visa_types.name: Malta Permanent Residence Programme (MPRP)
visa_types.purpose: Grants non-EU/EEA/Swiss nationals permanent residency status in Malta in exchange for a qualifying financial investment in real estate and a government contribution.
visa_types.notes: Grants permanent residency (not citizenship). UPDATED via Legal Notice 146 of 2025 (effective for applications from 1 January 2025). Key requirements: (1) Administration fee: €60,000 (€15,000 non-refundable upfront + €45,000 after Letter of Approval in Principle). (2) Government contribution: €37,000 (unified regardless of purchase or lease; replaces previous dual structure of €28,000 purchase / €58,000 rental). Spouse and minor children no longer attract a separate contribution fee. (3) Property: purchase min €375,000; OR lease min €14,000/year for at least 5 years. (4) NGO donation: €2,000. (5) Proof of capital: Option A - €500,000 total assets (min €150,000 financial); Option B - €650,000 total assets (min €75,000 financial). Assets monitored annually for first 5 years. (6) Adult dependant fee: €7,500 each. Residence certificate does not expire if obligations are maintained. Certain nationalities ineligible (Afghanistan, North Korea, Iran, DRC, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Yemen, Venezuela, Russia, Belarus). Regulated by S.L. 217.26.

visa_types.name: Malta Residence and Visa Programme (MRVP)
visa_types.purpose: Grants eligible non-EU/EEA/Swiss nationals and their families Maltese residency with Schengen travel rights through a qualifying property investment.
visa_types.notes: Regulated under S.L. 217.18. Grants Schengen travel rights with Maltese residency. Covers up to four generations of family members. Investment must be maintained for minimum 5 years. Full fee/investment schedule not publicly available on official pages - consult licensed agents or official MRVP FAQs PDF. Administered by Residency Malta Agency.

visa_types.name: Global Residence Programme (GRP)
visa_types.purpose: Tax-residency programme for non-EU/EEA/Swiss nationals providing a flat 15% tax rate on foreign-sourced income remitted to Malta, with a qualifying property purchase or lease requirement.
visa_types.notes: Administrative fee: €5,500 if qualifying property is in South Malta; €6,000 otherwise. Flat 15% tax on foreign-sourced income remitted to Malta; 35% on locally-sourced income. Applicants must purchase or lease qualifying property in Malta. Not available to those already long-term residents. Regulated by Malta Tax & Customs Administration (MTCA, formerly CFR). Commonly used as a retirement/passive-income residency route.

visa_types.name: Working Holiday Visa - Australia
visa_types.purpose: Allows Australian citizens aged 18–30 to live and work in Malta on a working holiday basis for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Exclusively for Australian biometric passport holders. Age: 18–30 at time of application. Visa issued free of charge by Maltese authorities; applicant pays VFS service fee (amount not published on official page). Non-renewable and one-time use only. Requires: valid biometric passport (90+ days beyond stay), comprehensive medical insurance (hospitalisation, liability, medical repatriation), proof of reasonable funds for initial stay, return ticket or funds to purchase one, no criminal record, no dependents, no prior Working Holiday Visa for Malta. Applications via VFS (Identità's External Service Provider).

visa_types.name: Working Holiday Visa - Andorra
visa_types.purpose: Allows Andorran citizens aged 18–30 to live and work in Malta on a working holiday basis for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Exclusively for Andorran biometric passport holders. Age: 18–30. Visa issued free of charge by Maltese authorities; applicant pays VFS service fee. Requires: valid biometric passport (90+ days beyond stay), comprehensive medical insurance, proof of funds, return ticket or funds, no criminal record, no dependents, no prior Malta working holiday visa. Applications via VFS.

visa_types.name: Working Holiday Visa - Japan
visa_types.purpose: Allows Japanese citizens aged 18–30 to live and work in Malta on a working holiday basis for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Exclusively for Japanese biometric passport holders. Age: 18–30. Visa issued free of charge by Maltese authorities; applicant pays VFS service fee. Requires: valid biometric passport (90+ days beyond stay), comprehensive medical insurance, proof of funds, return ticket or funds, no criminal record, no dependents, no prior Malta working holiday visa. Applications via VFS. Interviews may be conducted.

visa_types.name: Working Holiday Visa - New Zealand
visa_types.purpose: Allows New Zealand citizens aged 18–30 to live and work in Malta on a working holiday basis for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Exclusively for New Zealand biometric passport holders. Age: 18–30. Visa issued free of charge by Maltese authorities; applicant pays VFS service fee. Requires: valid biometric passport (90+ days beyond stay), comprehensive medical insurance (hospitalisation, liability, medical repatriation), proof of funds, return ticket or funds, no criminal record, no dependents, no prior Malta working holiday visa. Applications via VFS.

visa_types.name: Airport Transit Visa (ATV)
visa_types.purpose: Required for nationals of certain third countries to transit through the international zone of a Schengen airport without entering Schengen territory.
visa_types.notes: Airport Transit Visa (ATV) is a Schengen category for transit through the international transit area only (does not permit entry into Schengen territory). Required for nationals listed in Annex IV of EU Regulation 2018/1806 and national lists. Standard fee same as Schengen short-stay visa (€80 / ~USD 90). Not applicable to Schengen-area connecting flights without exit from international zone. Applications via VFS or Maltese diplomatic mission.

visa_types.name: Malta Startup Residence Programme
visa_types.purpose: Grants non-EU/EEA/Swiss startup founders, co-founders, and core employees residency in Malta to build and operate their startup business.
visa_types.notes: Founders/co-founders: 3-year permit renewable for up to 5 further years. Core employees: 3-year permit renewable for 3 further years. Startup must be registered no longer than 7 years globally and operate in eligible sectors (manufacturing, software, health/biotech, eco, or other innovative activities). Minimum paid-up share capital: €25,000 (plus €10,000 per additional co-founder beyond 4; max 6 co-founders). Core employees must earn min €30,000 gross annually. Business plan must be approved by Malta Enterprise. Application fee: €750/adult applicant for 3-year permit + €300 residence card issuance + €690 Jobsplus employment licence. Jointly administered by Residency Malta Agency and Malta Enterprise. Certain nationalities ineligible.

visa_types.name: Key Employee Initiative (KEI) Single Permit
visa_types.purpose: Fast-track single permit for non-EU nationals accepting managerial or highly technical positions in Malta, or roles in endorsed startups, with a minimum annual gross salary of €45,000.
visa_types.notes: Processing time: 5 working days from complete application submission. Initial permit valid 1 year; renewable up to 3 years. Fee: €600 for first application; €150/year for renewal. Minimum annual gross salary: €45,000. Employer must advertise the role for at least 2 weeks within 2 months prior to application. Managed by Identità's Expatriates Unit.

visa_types.name: Specialist Employee Initiative (SEI) Single Permit
visa_types.purpose: Single permit for non-EU nationals in skilled roles meeting minimum qualification and salary criteria (MQF Level 6+ or equivalent experience, minimum €30,000 gross annually).
visa_types.notes: For non-EU nationals not meeting KEI criteria. Minimum annual gross salary: €30,000. Requires MQF Level 6+ qualification OR lower-level qualification plus 3+ years relevant experience proven via contracts/references. Standard Single Permit process applies (processing time not specified). Managed by Identità's Expatriates Unit.

visa_types.name: EU Blue Card
visa_types.purpose: Residence and work permit for non-EU nationals with advanced professional qualifications and a high-salary job offer in Malta, issued under the EU Blue Card Directive.
visa_types.notes: Issued under the EU Blue Card Directive. Holders may move between EU member states after 18 months of legal residence. Managed by Identità's Expatriates Unit. Full eligibility requirements and fee schedule available from Identità.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic / Official Visa Exemption (Bilateral Treaties)
visa_types.purpose: Visa-free access for holders of diplomatic, official, service or special passports of qualifying countries under bilateral agreements or national derogations communicated to the EU Commission.
visa_types.notes: Malta has bilateral visa-waiver agreements for diplomatic/official/special passports with China (diplomatic: up to 30 days), Qatar (diplomatic/special), Pakistan (diplomatic/official). National derogations from the Schengen visa requirement also cover diplomatic/service/special passport holders from: Algeria (diplomatic), Bolivia (diplomatic/service), Egypt (diplomatic/service/special), India (diplomatic), Indonesia (diplomatic/service), Jordan (diplomatic), Kazakhstan (diplomatic), Morocco (diplomatic/service), Oman (diplomatic/service/special), Philippines (diplomatic/service), Thailand (diplomatic/service), Tunisia (diplomatic/service), Turkey (diplomatic/service). Source: EU OJ national derogations notice CELEX:52023XC0927(01) and individual bilateral treaty pages on foreign.gov.mt.
